name: The Georgia Department of Community Affairs
description_short: The Georgia Department of Community Affairs is a credit repair provider based in Atlanta, Georgia. Rated 1.9/5 with 163 Google reviews, reflecting limited customer satisfaction.
description_long:
The Georgia Department of Community Affairs is a credit repair provider serving clients in Atlanta, Georgia. With 163 Google reviews and a 1.9/5.0 rating, The Georgia Department of Community Affairs has built a significant presence in the local credit repair market.

The Georgia Department of Community Affairs specializes in credit repair services designed to help consumers repair and improve their credit scores. Additional information about their services and processes can typically be found on their website.

When evaluating credit repair services in Atlanta, consumers should look for providers that dispute directly with all three credit bureaus — Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion. Key differentiators include transparency around pricing, realistic timelines for results, and whether the company offers a money-back guarantee. The Georgia Department of Community Affairs operates in a highly regulated industry under the Credit Repair Organizations Act (CROA), which requires written contracts, a three-day cancellation window, and prohibits upfront fees before services are rendered.

Consumers in the Atlanta, Georgia area who are considering The Georgia Department of Community Affairs should contact the company directly to discuss their specific situation and get current pricing information. As with any credit repair provider, it is advisable to compare multiple options, verify licensing and credentials, and read recent customer reviews before making a decision.
